spec_match.rationale: Each .nav-item carries anchor-scope: --item-anchor and each trigger sets anchor-name: --item-anchor — the canonical pattern for a menu where every trigger reuses one name.

interactive.rationale: Real popover-based submenus, native popover API, aria-expanded sync, @supports fallback. Three triggers, three independent popovers.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card promises three triggers sharing --item-anchor with each popover opening beneath its OWN trigger; page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Has @supports fallback, scoped/unscoped comparison mode, measured submenu alignment readout, and popover toggle handlers wired.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables and design system colours throughout; .submenu now uses current `position-area` syntax with `inset-area` kept only as an older fallback.

summary: Re-tested with chrome-devtools-mcp in Chrome 148. Before the fix, Chrome supported `position-area: block-end span-inline-end` but not the authored `inset-area`, so every submenu computed `position-area: none` and opened at viewport origin. After the fix, all three scoped menus computed `position-area: end span-end`, kept `anchor-scope: --item-anchor`, opened with `aria-expanded=true`, and stayed at 0px horizontal offset from their own trigger. Switching the new scope mode to disabled set `anchor-scope: none`; Products drifted 186px and Docs 77px horizontally toward the Community trigger, while Community remained the aligned last trigger. The feature conformance route reported 8 PASS / 0 FAIL with no console errors.
